name: M2 Browning .50 Cal
prompt:
Vietnam War era M2 Browning .50 caliber heavy machine gun on M3 tripod mount. Used on firebases and vehicles.

Physical structure:
- Receiver: large rectangular receiver body, dark steel
- Barrel: long heavy barrel (~1.1m) with perforated barrel jacket/heat shroud
- Back plate: with dual spade grips (V-shaped handles at rear)
- Butterfly trigger: between the spade grips
- Feed tray: top-loading, accepts belted ammunition from left
- Ammo box: M2A1 ammunition can mounted on left side bracket, olive drab
- Ammo belt: visible belt of .50 cal rounds feeding into receiver
- M3 tripod: tubular steel tripod with:
  - Three splayed legs with ground plates
  - T&E mechanism (traverse and elevation) connecting tripod head to receiver
  - Pintle mount at center

Colors: Dark gun metal (0x2a2a30), olive drab ammo box (0x556B2F), brass belt (0xb5a642), dark tripod (0x333333)
Orientation: Mounted on tripod, barrel along +Z, ready to fire
Scale: Real-world meters (receiver ~1.6m with barrel)
animations: No animation needed for mounted weapon
